content.text: Looking at the baseline: BRAM18=0 with LUT=64530 and FF=5850 — everything is in distributed LUT-RAM (including the ~147 Kb cache_a/cache_b that should be BRAM). The likely cause is the `if/else` conditional read into a single shared register `cache_word_q2`, which can prevent Vivado from inferring BRAMs cleanly. The fix: split into two parallel registered reads plus a combinational output mux (canonical BRAM read pattern), which preserves cycle timing exactly.
